sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The player moved RIGHT from cols 32-35 to cols 36-38, and the shape changed slightly (rows 40-43 instead of 41-43). The player seems to be a 4x3 block now. Also notice the player's color changed from N (light green) to... wait, it's still N (id=9, color=N). But the old position (id=10) is now white (W), which is the trail/previous position.
So the player leaves a white trail behind. The player moved right, and the old position became white.
Now the player is at rows 40-43, cols 36-38. The box at 36-39, 32-35 is to the left and slightly above. Let me think about navigation.
The layout:
- Box 1: [24-27, 44-47] (top-right) with blue center
- Box 2: [28-31, 16-19] (left) with blue center  
- Box 3: [36-39, 32-35] (center) with blue center
- Horizontal bar: [28-31, 28-39] with gray interior
- Player: [40-43, 36-38]
The player needs to navigate around these obstacles. Let me try to go UP now that I'm to the right of the center box. The center box is at cols 32-35 and player is at cols 36-38, so there's space to go up.
Actually wait - the horizontal bar is at [28-31, 28-39]. The player is at cols 36-38, which overlaps with the bar's columns. So going UP might hit the horizontal bar.
Let me think about the path:
- Player at 40-43, 36-38
- Need to reach box 1 at 24-27, 44-47 or box 2 at 28-31, 16-19
- The horizontal bar at 28-31, 28-39 blocks the middle
For box 1 (top-right): need to go UP and RIGHT. The bar blocks UP at cols 28-39. So need to go RIGHT first to get past col 39, then UP.
For box 2 (left): need to go UP and LEFT. The bar doesn't extend to col 16-19, so could potentially go LEFT and then UP.
Let me try going RIGHT more to get past the horizontal bar, then UP toward the top-right box.

sections.content:

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at 40-43, 36-38
# Horizontal bar at 28-31, 28-39 blocks UP
# Need to go RIGHT past col 39 to clear the bar
# Let me try multiple RIGHTs to get past the bar

result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Green at rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
